name: Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Melonares desde Almadén de la Plata – circular por el Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
description: Cycle a difficult 30.1-mile circular route from Almadén de la Plata through Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park to Embalse de Melonares.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almadén De La Plata is characterized by its diverse landscapes within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features dehesas, Mediterranean scrubland, and geological formations, including granite outcrops. Rivers such as the Rivera de Cala and Viar contribute to the scenic routes, with the Viar River notable for its deep fault lines. The area also incorporates sections of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Almadén De La Plata The most popular touring cycling route is Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop, a 30.0 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete, passing by the Melonares reservoir within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata – Amphitheatre of Italica loop from Almadén de la Plata, a difficult 78.0 miles (125.6 km) path that follows a segment of the ancient Roman road, offering a blend of historical and natural scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Almadén de la Plata – Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from Almadén de la Plata, a 30.9 miles (49.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Touring cycling around Almadén De La Plata is defined by dehesas, river valleys, and the historic Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging long-distance routes to shorter loops. The routes in Almadén De La Plata are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almadén De La Plata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almadén De La Plata?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Almadén De La Plata, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.75 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The terrain around Almadén De La Plata is diverse, characterized by &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (Mediterranean woodlands), Mediterranean scrubland, and unique geological formations, including granite outcrops. You'll also find routes along river valleys like the Rivera de Cala and Viar River, with some sections incorporating the historic Vía de la Plata, which offers a mix of off-road and paved surfaces.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Almadén De La Plata?
A: While many routes are rated as 'difficult' due to elevation changes and distance, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. It's always recommended to check the route details on komoot for specific difficulty ratings, elevation profiles, and distances to find a tour that matches your fitness level.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can discover while cycling?
A: The region offers several stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for expansive panoramic vistas of the Sierra. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along the Arroyo de la Calzadilla is another beautiful spot, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt; adds to the scenic beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991641024/almaden-de-la-plata-and-melonares-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, passes by the Melonares reservoir within the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Almadén De La Plata?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Almadén De La Plata are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991641024/almaden-de-la-plata-and-melonares-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata and Melonares Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata – Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Almadén de la Plata is rich in history. You can explore sections of the ancient Roman road, the Vía de la Plata, which is part of the Camino de Santiago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata – Amphitheatre of Italica loop from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a route that follows a segment of this historic path. Additionally, the area features medieval castle remains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt;, and archaeological sites like a megalithic complex.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, where Almadén de la Plata is located, offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes with seasonal wildflowers. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while generally mild, can bring cooler temperatures.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while cycling in the region?
A: Yes, the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park is known for its diverse animal species. Cyclists often have the opportunity to observe wildlife, including deer, especially in the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and Mediterranean scrubland areas. The natural environment provides a rich habitat for various birds and other animals.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a good challenge for advanced touring cyclists?
A: For advanced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata – Amphitheatre of Italica loop from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. This route is rated as 'difficult' and covers a significant distance with considerable elevation gain, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling tour?
A: Almadén de la Plata, as a base for exploring the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, typically offers parking options within the town or at designated trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage or visitor information centers upon arrival for the most convenient and suitable parking areas for cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Almadén De La Plata, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical routes like the Vía de la Plata, and the sense of adventure offered by the varied terrain.
tips: The Sierra Norte Natural Park, declared a Geopark in 2011 (European Geoparks Network), is a protected natural space with valuable natural resources. These mountains, which form part of the Sierra Morena, are dominated by meadow forests of holm oaks and cork oaks.

The Mirador Cerro del Calvario is a beautiful observation point located in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, near Almadén de la Plata. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the region and is a popular hiking spot.
The route to the viewpoint is moderate and accessible for most fitness levels. Also near the viewpoint is El Chorro Waterfall, another natural attraction worth visiting.
An incredible valley in the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla that's best enjoyed at dawn or dusk.
Views of the Melonares reservoir, which was built under the pressure of the 1992-1996 drought.
It's a nice, pleasant five kilometer climb from the swamp. The views are beautiful, it is worth a stop at the top and enjoy them.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring cycling route, covering approximately 48.4 km, is estimated to take around 4 hours and 17 minutes of active cycling time. However, allow for additional time to enjoy the scenic viewpoints and explore Almadén de la Plata.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte, which is mostly flat and easy with a mixed surface. However, venturing beyond the Vía Verde, especially on roads connecting to towns like Cazalla de la Sierra, involves narrow, mountainous asphalt roads with significant elevation changes, including climbs like the Puerto Padrona-Puerto Quejigo. Near the Embalse de Melonares, you'll find well-surfaced roads with a steep but comfortable final ascent towards Almadén de la Plata.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance (48.4 km), and considerable elevation gain (972 meters), this route is generally not recommended for beginners. It combines easy greenway sections with more demanding, mountainous road cycling that requires a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Almadén de la Plata, which serves as an excellent base. You should be able to find parking within the town, as it's a common starting point for exploring the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the diverse beauty of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, characterized by cork oak and holm oak forests, particularly along the Vía Verde. Expect views of fertile lands in the Viar Valley, wild dehesa, and expansive water views of the Melonares Reservoir. The route also passes through charming landscapes with white villages.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Section Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla itself, the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint, and various scenic spots offering Views from the path to Almadén. You'll also get stunning perspectives of the reservoir from the View of Melonares Reservoir and the View of Melonares Reservoir from the Climb. The Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte, with its old railway tunnels and restored stations, is also a notable feature.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Transandalus, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, Camino a Almadén, and Los Arrianales.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park is best enjoyed in the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for cycling, and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making cycling challenging.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas and near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Be mindful of the route's difficulty and length when bringing a dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park for activities like cycling. However, if you plan any specific activities or want to access certain restricted areas, it's always advisable to check with the park's official website or visitor centers for any specific regulations or permits that might apply.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: Almadén de la Plata, your starting and ending point, offers amenities like restaurants and cafes where you can resupply or enjoy a meal. The town is also on the Silver Route of the Camino de Santiago, so you might find pilgrim's menus. Beyond Almadén, options might be limited, so it's wise to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the route that offer a unique experience or challenge?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The sections utilizing the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte offer a unique, easy-going experience on a converted old railway line, complete with tunnels and restored stations. In contrast, the climbs and descents on the mountainous asphalt roads, particularly towards the reservoir, provide a more challenging and rewarding cycling experience with panoramic views. The contrast between these two types of terrain is a defining feature of this route.
